questionText: Describe a situation where you had a conflict with a teammate. How did you resolve it?

starFramework.Situation: Describe a work context where a difference of opinions on a design or priority occurred.
starFramework.Task: Identify the cause of friction and how it risked impacting the project milestones.
starFramework.Action: Detail how you scheduled a 1-on-1, separated opinions from objective metrics, and focused on target results.
starFramework.Result: Explain how you resolved the conflict, reached a consensus, and improved the relationship.

expectedPoints: Focused on objective data rather than emotions
commonMistakes: Portraying yourself as completely right and the teammate as wrong
Involving management immediately before attempting 1-on-1 resolution
strongExample: We were designing a service architecture. A colleague preferred standard REST while I wanted gRPC for payload efficiency. Instead of arguing, we set up a micro-benchmark project. The data showed gRPC saved 40% bandwidth but REST saved 20% dev time. Since our budget was tight but latency wasn't critical, we compromised on REST with JSON compression, maintaining project velocity.
weakExample: A peer changed my code without telling me. I was angry and told them to revert it. They said their way was better. We didn't talk for a week until our lead stepped in.
